creator: salvadore@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Mark Millard from comment #18)

In general it is a good idea to merge into the quarterly branch build fixes. However, in this case,

- the patch has been committed with a 'weak' maintainer approval only. So, while we know builds are fine, we do not know if the patch could also have introduced some bad side effect at runtime;

- as far as I know, there is no demand for edk2 on aarch64: indeed this PR has been opened to submit the patch directly, not because someone needed to run on edk2 on aarch64.

Hence, I believe that there is no hurry to merge quarterly and we can wait the next quarterly branch.
